documentation_accuracy_summary: doc/synthesized-roadmap.md's H15 row (High-impact defects table) has exactly one line changed: a bolded '**[RESOLVED -- see P2-6: ...; see artifacts/phase2-rules-correctness/P2-6.]**' clause appended to the end of the existing Detail cell, mirroring the Phase 1 C1-C6 / P2-3 S2-S3 annotation pattern. Confirmed via git diff 37fb564b..HEAD -- doc/synthesized-roadmap.md that no other line in the file was touched.

The RESOLVED annotation's summary text accurately matches the shipped behavior verified above: 'onSave()/onOpen() now check wxFileDialog::ShowModal() and treat Cancel/any non-OK result as a no-op (no stream opened, no FGame created or loaded), and use the dialog's full GetPath() instead of the cwd-dependent GetFilename() on a confirmed OK; onOpen()'s post-load menu-enable / turn-state logic is unchanged on the success path' -- verified against the actual src/FMainFrame.cpp diff line-by-line.

include/FMainFrame.h Doxygen headers for onSave()/onOpen() (lines 90-123) were updated to describe the Cancel-is-a-no-op / GetPath() behavior, correctly preserve the original @author (Tom Stephens) while appending 'Claude Sonnet 5 (medium)', and update @date Last Modified to Jul 11, 2026 (today's date) -- matches AGENTS.md's Doxygen author/date conventions exactly.
tests/gui/StrategicGuiLiveTest.h Doxygen for the 4 new test methods and the class-level header follow the same author/date pattern and accurately describe each test's assertion.

doc/rules/tactical_operations_manual.md and doc/rules/second-sathar-war-rules.md (non-editable per AGENTS.md) were not touched -- confirmed via git diff --stat 37fb564b..HEAD -- doc/rules/ (empty output).
No contradictions or duplicated facts found between the roadmap annotation, the Doxygen comments, and the actual implementation.
test_sufficiency_summary: All 4 acceptance criteria are backed by dedicated BEHAVIORAL GUI tests in tests/gui/StrategicGuiLiveTest.cpp that construct a real FMainFrame, drive the real wxFileDialog through a native GtkFileChooser responder (NativeFileDialogResponder), and assert on observed runtime state (filesystem existence/size, live wxMenuItem enable state) -- not source-text or header-declaration inspection.
testMainFrameOnSaveCancelLeavesFilesystemUntouched (tests/gui/StrategicGuiLiveTest.cpp:2073-2096): asserts a reserved absolute path does not exist after a Cancel-driven onSave().

testMainFrameOnSaveConfirmWritesToDialogFullPath (tests/gui/StrategicGuiLiveTest.cpp:2098-2130): asserts a sentinel-seeded file at the dialog's GetPath() is truncated to 0 bytes after a confirm-driven onSave().
testMainFrameOnOpenCancelCreatesNoGameAndLeavesFrameConsistent (tests/gui/StrategicGuiLiveTest.cpp:2132-2163): asserts all game-dependent menu items remain disabled after a Cancel-driven onOpen().

testMainFrameOnOpenConfirmLoadsFromFullPathAndRestoresPostLoadState (tests/gui/StrategicGuiLiveTest.cpp:2165-2233): asserts the post-load menu/turn-state block ran correctly (Sathar-turn state) after loading a real saved game from GetPath().

Independently re-verified the Tester's disclosed red/green claim rather than accepting it on trust: reverted src/FMainFrame.cpp to the pre-fix version (git show 37fb564b), rebuilt tests/gui/GuiTests, and reran the StrategicGuiLiveTest fixture under xvfb-run/timeout -- the run terminated with 'timeout: the monitored command dumped core' (a genuine segfault), corroborating the Tester's gdb-reported SIGSEGV in FGame::load->FMap::load->FSystem::load->FPlanet::load when onOpen() unconditionally creates an FGame and calls load() on an empty/garbage Cancel stream. Restored the fixed source (diff against HEAD is empty afterward) and reran make -C tests/gui plus the full GUI suite: OK (75 tests) again, confirming no residual state corruption from the temporary revert.

This confirms testMainFrameOnOpenCancelCreatesNoGameAndLeavesFrameConsistent is a genuine must-fail-against-unfixed-code behavioral test, satisfying AGENTS.md's Behavioral Verification requirement for at least the primary (crash-risk) defect.

The Tester's report (artifacts/phase2-rules-correctness/P2-6/tester_report.md) transparently discloses that the other three tests (Save Cancel, Save Confirm, Open Confirm) are green against both pre-fix and post-fix code in the specific harness-observable scenario: (a) Save Cancel -- a cancelled native dialog returns an empty GetFilename(), so the pre-fix std::ofstream("") also fails to open, leaving the filesystem untouched either way; (b) both Confirm cases -- wxFD_CHANGE_DIR chdir's the process into the chosen file's folder on accept, so the pre-fix GetFilename() (basename resolved against the new cwd) happens to resolve to the same absolute path as the fixed GetPath() in this exact test scenario. This is a real, disclosed limitation of headless native-dialog testing, not an attempt to hide weak coverage, and it does not defeat the requirement: each of these three is still a genuine behavioral test (exercises the real dialog/file/menu state, not source text) and functions as a durable post-fix contract/regression lock (e.g. it would catch a regression that broke the OK-branch's file targeting, or reintroduced acting on Cancel in a way that touches the filesystem).

Verdict on the behavioral-coverage question posed by the task: the coverage is adequate. One criterion (Open Cancel) has independently-reproduced, gdb/core-dump-confirmed red/green discrimination against the unfixed code -- satisfying the non-negotiable must-fail-against-unfixed bar for the change's highest-risk defect (a crash). All four tests are true behavioral tests (real dialog, real filesystem, real menu state), and the three that coincide pre/post-fix do so for well-understood, disclosed structural reasons (empty-cancel-path semantics and wxFD_CHANGE_DIR path equivalence) rather than because they were shallow or mis-targeted. No BLOCKING test-sufficiency finding.

No missing edge cases identified relative to the plan's 4 acceptance criteria: onSave()'s if(m_game) guard on the confirm path (unchanged pre-existing behavior, not part of this subtask's scope) is exercised implicitly by the Confirm test with m_game == NULL, and correctly produces a 0-byte truncated file rather than a crash or written garbage.
